title: Reading Data Like a Spreadsheet
summary: Learn how to read data from a SQL database as if it were a spreadsheet.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to SQL Tables
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, data is organized into tables, much like a spreadsheet. Each table has rows and columns. For example, consider the `students` table:

```
| id | name | grade | age | city     |
|----|------|-------|-----|----------|
| 1  | Noor | A     | 15  | Chicago  |
| 2  | Mia  | A     | 14  | Evanston |
| 3  | Leo  | B     | 15  | Chicago  |
| 4  | Ava  | C     | 14  | Oak Park |
| 5  | Zane | B     | 16  | Naperville |
```

Each row represents a student, and each column represents an attribute of the student, such as their name or grade. Try it yourself by visualizing how you would organize your own data in a table format.

sketchBlocks.title: Basic SQL SELECT Statement
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
The `SELECT` statement is used to retrieve data from a database. It allows you to specify which columns you want to see. For example, to view the names and grades of all students, you would write:

```
SELECT name, grade FROM students;
```

This query selects the `name` and `grade` columns from the `students` table. Try it yourself: Write a query to select the `name` and `city` columns from the `students` table.

quizDraft.title: Understanding SQL Tables
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following best describes a SQL table?
options: A collection of related data organized in rows and columns.
quizDraft.hint: Think about how data is structured in a spreadsheet.

help.concept: A SQL table is similar to a spreadsheet, organizing data into rows and columns.
help.hint_1: Consider how data is organized in a table format.
help.hint_2: A table contains multiple rows and columns, not just a single piece of data.

quizDraft.title: SQL SELECT Statement
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are true about the SQL SELECT statement? (Choose all that apply)
options: It retrieves data from a database.
It modifies data in a database.
It can specify which columns to display.
It deletes data from a database.
quizDraft.hint: Focus on the purpose of the SELECT statement.

help.concept: The SELECT statement is used to retrieve and display data from a database.
help.hint_1: SELECT is used for retrieving, not modifying or deleting data.
help.hint_2: SELECT can specify columns to display, but not modify or delete data.

quizDraft.title: Order of SQL SELECT Statement
quizDraft.prompt: Arrange the parts of a SQL SELECT statement in the correct order.
quizDraft.hint: Think about the basic structure of a SELECT statement.

help.concept: The SELECT statement begins with SELECT, followed by columns, then FROM, and finally the table name.
help.hint_1: SELECT comes first, followed by the columns you want to display.
help.hint_2: After SELECT and columns, FROM specifies the table.

quizDraft.title: SQL SELECT Syntax
quizDraft.prompt: Complete the SQL statement to select the name and grade from the students table: `SELECT [blank1] FROM students;`
quizDraft.hint: Think about which columns you want to display.

help.concept: The SELECT statement specifies which columns to display.
help.hint_1: You want to display the name and grade columns.
help.hint_2: The correct columns are name and grade.

quizDraft.title: SQL Table Columns
quizDraft.prompt: Which column is NOT part of the students table?
quizDraft.hint: Review the columns in the students table.

help.concept: The students table includes columns for name, grade, age, and city.
help.hint_1: Think about the columns that are relevant to student data.
help.hint_2: Salary is not a column in the students table.

quizDraft.title: SQL Table Structure
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following columns are part of the students table? (Choose all that apply)
quizDraft.hint: Refer to the schema of the students table.

help.concept: The students table includes columns for id, name, grade, age, and city.
help.hint_1: Focus on columns that relate to student information.
help.hint_2: Salary is not a column in the students table.

quizDraft.title: SQL Table Row Order
quizDraft.prompt: Arrange the students by their id in ascending order.
quizDraft.hint: Order the students by their id numbers.

help.concept: The id column represents the unique identifier for each student.
help.hint_1: Start with the smallest id number.
help.hint_2: Arrange the names according to their id order.

quizDraft.title: SQL Column Selection
quizDraft.prompt: To select the age and city from the students table, complete the statement: `SELECT [blank1] FROM students;`
quizDraft.hint: Identify the columns you want to display.

help.concept: The SELECT statement specifies which columns to display.
help.hint_1: You want to display the age and city columns.
help.hint_2: The correct columns are age and city.
